title: Inbound Receiving
description: Standard for goods receiving: from truck arrival to WMS check-in.

steps.title: Pre-arrival preparation
steps.content: 30 minutes before arrival, check in WMS: supplier, PO number, SKU list and quantities, payment terms (on delivery / net 30), unloading zone. Reserve the dock, prep equipment (pallet jack, forklift), people (minimum 2 warehouse workers + receiver). Unprepared = truck idle = supplier penalties from the store.
steps.key_takeaway: 30 minutes prep. Truck shouldn't wait more than 15 minutes.

steps.title: Meeting the driver and checking paperwork
steps.content: Truck pulls in: verify BOL (bill of lading) and invoice. Must match: truck number, seal, consignor, item list, piece count. Driver signature on the BOL is mandatory. If seal is broken — don't unload, photograph, draft an incident report with driver signature, call the manager.
steps.key_takeaway: Broken seal = report + call. Unloading without check = unprovable later.

steps.title: External cargo inspection
steps.content: Before opening truck doors: inspect boxes through the opening. Crushed packaging, spills, odor — grounds for a report. Inside the truck: temperature (for dairy, frozen — mandatory data logger and pyrometer check), humidity, contamination. Photo-document anything problematic BEFORE unloading.
steps.key_takeaway: Photos before unloading. Proving damage happened in transit after the fact is nearly impossible.

steps.title: Unloading piece-by-piece
steps.content: Unload one pallet at a time, count boxes immediately, not 'we'll count later'. Count in the driver's presence. Quantity discrepancy = immediately logged on BOL ('received 98 cases vs 100 on manifest'), driver signature + yours. No signature = shortage on you.
steps.key_takeaway: Discrepancies logged in driver's presence. Driver leaves = no claim possible.

steps.title: Quality inspection
steps.content: Sample quality check: open 5-10% of boxes from the batch, verify contents. Expiry dates (minimum 70% remaining shelf life per contract), package integrity, SKU match. Damaged — to a separate zone, damage report with a photo of each unit. Don't mix damaged with sellable stock.
steps.key_takeaway: Damaged goes to a separate zone, separate report. Mixed with good = whole pallet goes back.

steps.title: WMS check-in
steps.content: In the WMS (SAP, Oracle, proprietary): create a receiving note based on the BOL. Scan barcode of each box (or pallet for pallet-receiving). Specify storage zone: main bin, picking zone, quarantine for new suppliers. Confirm receipt — only then the stock 'exists' in the system and becomes available for orders.
steps.key_takeaway: Stock exists physically but not in WMS = might as well not exist. Orders won't form, sales are lost.

steps.title: Putaway and paperwork
steps.content: Distribute stock across zones: fast-movers closer to shipping, heavy items low, light items high, fragile separately. ABC principle: A-items (20% SKUs, 80% sales) — the most accessible location. File BOL in the shift log, forward a copy to accounting within 24 hours.
steps.key_takeaway: A-items to the golden spot. BOL to accounting within 24 hours, or the supplier doesn't get paid.

quiz.question: What to do if the truck seal is broken?
options: Don't unload, draft a report, call the manager
quiz.explanation: Unloading without a report = impossible to prove where the damage occurred.

quiz.question: When to log a quantity discrepancy?
options: In the driver's presence, with their signature
quiz.explanation: Without driver signature, a supplier claim is legally impossible.

quiz.question: Sample inspection percentage?
quiz.explanation: 5-10% is a statistically sufficient sample to catch systematic defects.

quiz.question: What is the ABC principle for putaway?
options: A-items (fast-movers) to the most accessible spot
quiz.explanation: A-items drive 80% of sales. Faster access = higher picker productivity.

quiz.question: BOL submission deadline to accounting?
quiz.explanation: Delay = delayed supplier payment = strained relationship.

manager_checklist: BOL and invoice reconciled, seal verified
Photos of cargo condition BEFORE unloading
Discrepancies logged with driver present and signed
Damaged goods in a separate zone with a report
BOL forwarded to accounting within 24 hours

kpi.how_to_measure: WMS: receiving note vs BOL reconciliation
Procurement report on returns and chargebacks
